reviews.review_text: We stayed at Rio Rita Caravan Park for 5 nights on a powered caravan site and had the best time. The park is very neat and clean and spacious. The location is excellent with walking distance to the heart of Bateman Bay just over the bridge way and a hop and step to the water edge. We loved the peaceful atmosphere. Would definitely stay again.
Had the best time at RioRita caravan park, we stayed in the bungalows and it was the best experience, so clean, cosy and had everything we need with the most beautiful view. 10/10 would recommend!

Awesome caravan park, beautiful grass areas with power and water available. Owner was wonderful in assisting us as a group with 3 caravans so we could camp together on a busy public holiday. Highly recommend :)
Such a beautiful park, was soo affordable during peak time and super friendly. Highly recommended.

We’ve just stayed at Rio Rita for 8 nights! From our first communication with Debbie regarding our dogs being welcome everything was seamless, Then when we wanted to extend our stay, once again there was no problem. |We didn’t use the amenities however we did use the dump point, it’s old school but worked perfectly well. |If you want a homely feeling with your stay then you won’t be disappointed. |Everyone’s needs and expectations are different but Rio Rita suited us and would highly recommend for a quiet and peaceful stay. |Doggie beach across the road and a nice park/reserve 300m away. |Well maintained lawns and lovely green grass.

We booked one of the cabins, perfect little spot, inside the cabin was perfect for us, sad that location wasnt closer to the main town but its only a taxi away. Also found an ear plug with the tea and coffee, we had a little giggle, loved the experience, thank you
Awesome staff members, great location site is very well looked after
Quaint old school caravan park, love it

We all camped - 4 separate sites.|Owner introduced herself - asked if we had received the codes and map and sent us on our way.  Sites not clearly marked; vans packed in like sardines; little to no shade.|Amenities where not clean; twice experienced cold showers; no toilet paper at one stage. |Location was good.

We booked a powered site for mid January 2026 and prior to this day had never heard of Rio Rita Caravan Park. We stayed four nights and could not fault the place. The owners are very present and extremely friendly. Our awning almost blew over and would have been destroyed had it not been for the park owners who saved us thousands of dollars and heartache.

We had never swam at the beach across the road and were pleasantly surprised with how calm, clean and lovely it was. There was adequate shade and it was right in front of the caravan park. We felt no need to go elsewhere for a swim.

The price per night was much cheaper than other places. The facilities are adequate, perhaps if you had children you may look for a place with a park and swimming pool but we enjoyed the quiet and modest setting of the place, which was immaculately kept and facilities were very clean.

We will definitely be booking this park for our next stay in the bay. It will actually be our preferred location.

We had a fantastic stay in a nicely appointed tiny home which very cosy and comfy. The lovely staff were so friendly and helpful. |The park is perfectly located on the Clyde River for walks along the sandy shore, views of the waters and Gateway Islands. We loved walking over the scenic bridge to cafes, restaurants and shops. |We stayed here with friends to experience the amazing mountain biking adventures of Mogo and Narooma. We’ll be back for more, plus the whale watching and to wet a line soon.

When we arrived they had an area taped off because of asbestos which I found a little concerning. The showers were cold and some of the other campers were loud until after 2am so it wasn’t a great start but it is really close to a pet friendly beach which was handy. Debbie was incredibly rude but the manager was really helpful. Recommended for out of season stop overs only.

We’ve just stayed at Rio Rita Caravan Park for the second time and once again had a great experience. We had a powered site which was well set up and in a nicely maintained area.

The bathrooms and shower facilities were excellent — clean, modern, and well looked after. The park itself is very tidy and peaceful, and you can tell a lot of care goes into maintaining it.

The owners are friendly, welcoming, and always happy to help. We had no issues at all during our stay and would absolutely stay here again. Highly recommended.

We’ve just stayed at Rio Rita Caravan Park for the second time and once again had a great experience. We had a powered site which was well set up and in a nicely maintained area.||The bathrooms and shower facilities were excellent — clean, modern, and well looked after. The park itself is very tidy and peaceful, and you can tell a lot of care goes into maintaining it.||The owners are friendly, welcoming, and always happy to help. We had no issues at all during our stay and would absolutely stay here again. Highly recommended.
We enjoyed our stay.

Stayed at Rio Rita caravan park in Bateman’s Bay! The location is perfect—close to town and right beside the river. The facilities, including showers and toilets, are excellent, and the views are absolutely beautiful. Debbie and Tony were amazing hosts, making our stay even more enjoyable. I would highly recommend this park to anyone visiting the area!
Stayed in a studio van & it had everything we needed for the weekend. Nicely styled with it's own bathroom, kitchenette & A/C.

Self check in is handy knowing your site location ahead of time. Park quiet but spotlessly clean amenities, the camp kitchen is equipped with all you might need. Good location with paths and bike tracks to town.
Excellent spot, only a short walk to waters edge. Our cabin was immaculate and great value for money. Spent a few days riding the MTB trails in the area, great location to explore the south coast.

Awesome little park
Went down for a weekend of mountain biking and was very happy with the friendly reception and the proximity to all the trails
Cheers
See you again soon 👌

Camping review:

We booked here for one night, early October and were really pleasantly surprised. We were the only people in the camping area apart from a few caravans. Amenities were dated but very clean with powerful shower and lots of hot water.

We stayed two nights in a unpowered site. Facilities were very clean and lovely hot water pressure in the showers. A very quiet campsite, lovely and peaceful.
Very accommodating with our booking and very responsive.
A short walk into the town where you have everything you could need.
We walked a short 2 min walk to the waters edge for sunset which was beautiful.
Would return if in the area again.
Very quiet when we stayed clean facility's great service

The facilities for a Gday park leave a lot to be desired |The toilets themselves are fine. The showers have leaking heads and minimal hot water.  |There is no divider in the shower so you cannot keep your clothing dry while you have a shower  - no shelf to put anything on within the cubical |With one rubbish dump right down the back of the park  …. |For the price point, this park is definitely overpriced

🤣🤣 No 😂 just No 🙅🏼‍♀️

Very rude and extremely unprofessional considering they've been running it since 1970's,, but hey maybe that's why 🤭🤭
Just saying 🤷🏼‍♀️

Why would you bother.
So unprofessional, rude and very violent
If youd like a holiday in the Bronx of the Bay this is your one stop trip to hell..
As for the beach across the road and shady forest it is not for the faint hearted or family orientated trust me drive 2 seconds down the road to a paradise park..

Had an amazing time with my friends at Rio Rita, we camped on site and the owners were very friendly and made sure we were comfortable and gave great recommendations. The site is well maintained with lots of hot water and a great location- right by the water. No complaints! Will come back soon 😊
P.s visit the oyster shack just down the road and make sure to do one of the coastal hikes nearby!!!
Lovely place!!! Staff are very kind and amenities nice and clean. Very close to beaches, walks, town and the oyster shack. Highly recommend
Nothing fancy, but the back sites are great and the price is fantastic.

Nice family run park, we spent a week here in our motorhome. Very quiet at this time of year. No drive through sites, but there is a couple of options near the camp kitchen where you could stay hooked up if you want. There is a large grassed area at back of park for camping. Amenities were quite good.

Loved our one night stopover at this caravan park. We choose an unpowered site as we are self contained and also didn’t use the ammenities. |It was dog friendly and so suitable for our little cavalier. |We had ample room with a lovely grassy site in the shade. A bonus was the lovely beach across the road only a minutes walk away. It was so quiet and we had no noise at all. The front of the park had permanent residents but the back grassy area was ideal for us and the  price was cheaper than the Big4 caravan park next door that didn’t allow pets. Also next door vans were packed in like sardines. |We will stay here again for sure and recommend to others travelling with pets.

Lovely older style caravan park. |Nothing flash, but clean amenities, friendly owners & great location.|Would return to this park.|Is pet friendly which is most important to us.|Good location, walk across to bay beach, easy walk across big bridge into town (Approximately 20 minutes).

Nice green grass across the site. Good Clean facilities.  Great location in Batemans Bay. Would stay here again. Owners were helpful and accommodating. Cool little Beach across the road. Awesome bar & restaurant on the side of the water walking distance.

Good communication with Debbie (owner). Camper van spots were a bit busy during Xmass time, but still quiet - clean showers, nice covered BBQ area. Small clean beach very near, not the best but good.
Powered site - Campervan.
Thanks Deb & Don for the wonderfoul time in your campingground. You was very helpfoul and kind to us! Our Studio was amazing!

We stayed at Rio Rita caravan park ror 4 days and really enjoyed our stay. The location is good and quiet and far enough away from the town for a good nights sleep. The beach is 50 metres away from our powered site and the sunrises were spectacular. The family run business was a pleasure as they would help out where they could. Good spot for a base and plenty to explore around the area. Big plus is that it is dog friendly.

Great service.
Amazing place.
Highly recommended.
The caravan park was quiet, and had friendly staff. Had two amenities blocks, plus a laundry for campers. Great beach access with it being right across the road from park. We enjoyed our stay here.

A great little gem of a caravan park 👌⛱️👍 Old school family run. Very casual 😎 Nothing flash but clean toilets and showers and responsive staff if you ring them... the office is rarely open.
Just across the road from a lovely, quiet, and sheltered beach 🏖 Doggies 🐕 welcome 😁. Just across the bridge from the main town and shops.
No frills or rides so bring your bikes kid's 👌
Must be very quiet most of the year as mostly permanent holiday shacks and some basic units
Powered site for campervan. Great communication with Debbie (owner). Super powerful shower, nice covered BBQ area. Grocery store ten minutes walk/two minutes drive away. Beach also. Very handy base.

We enjoyed our long weekend stay at Rio Rita, the hosts were so lovely and accommodating.
We camped with our friends and they were able to give us  spots close to each other.
Our kids had great time riding their scooters and bikes around the caravan park. The park and couple of beaches are all short minute walks. We were also able to visit the Sunday market in Batemans bay town.
The showers and toilets are very well maintained and clean.

Perfect caravan park ! Close to town and very green ! We stayed in our 22.6” caravan with lots of room and quiet . Will definitely come back here .
Also dog friendly
Great customer service from the hosts !
Loved our 5 night stay .

Der Caravanpark liegt direkt an der Hafeneinfahrt.
Der Platz selber ist super gemütlich, sehr sauber und die Betreuung ist hervorragend!
Zusammenfassend kann man einfach sagen:
Sehr zu empfehlen!
The caravan park is located right at the harbor entrance.
The site itself is super cozy, very clean and the service is excellent!
To sum up, you can simply say:
Highly recommended!

The worst customer service! Even though they knew we were arriving Sunday afternoon there was no-one there. There was a sign on the office door saying to text their number and they would allocate a site. We waited, we rang the number and no response, so we picked a site ourselves.     Finally, 3 hours later, we got a text, but we stayed where we were. The whole 3 days we didn't see a soul except for other confused campers. Anyone could just drive in and camp and they wouldn't know. BTW barbecues were filthy.

We loved stayed at Rio Rita Caaravan Park. We usually choose to stay at Big 4 / Ingenia , but this time we booked Rio Rita. We completely enjoyed the stay. It is a medium sized caravan park, perfect location , just cross the road for a beautiful beach , 5 mins walk to IGA , 4 mins drive to woollies , Coles , Kmart and other shops and restaurants , don’t miss the 5 mins walk to Eastern facing beach opposite to IGA for a splendid sunrise . The caravan park is very safe , a lovely camp kitchen and bathroom and toilets were very clean and well maintained. There were 3 bathrooms and 3 toilets , I thought they would be busy always , but it was always available and clean. The people were friendly and lovely at Batemans Bay. Overall , had an amazing experience. Turned out to be a great experience- Don’t judge a book by its cover .

Recommendations- would be nice if the kitchen and fridge could be maintained well and clean.

The CP is older and has little to offer families with children (no pool, playground, games room), however,  it is a perfect little park for couples, singles and families wanting to be by the beach without paying a fortune. Quiet and peaceful, the staff are lovely and friendly. The bathroom and laundry (although old and dated) are the cleanest we have used in over 12 months on the road. Well done to the staff who work hard to keep this park clean, fresh and well maintained.

very welcoming owners and
close to the shopping centers and walking distance to a great park and beach

Amenities were below average and not clean.  There was a shower running that could not be turned off.  The microwave in the camp kitchen didn’t work.  Reception was not staffed because she was too busy trying to do the cleaning.  A permanent resident was rude to my husband in the bathroom area.  There was a person just sitting around in the ladies bathroom.  Don’t stay here.  It’s like they are marking time until a developer buys the property.

This place is a disgrace- do not be fooled by the magnificent photos on the website!!  The amenities were not cleaned for days, the rubbish bins were so full it was clear that no one was on site to take care of the day to day necessities.   We witnessed so many people driving in and driving away _ I only wish we had the opportunity- but we pre-paid!!
Easy booking , lovely clean site , spotless showers and toilets. Near to beach and ten minutes over the bridge into town.

Wow! Love, love love it here!!! We booked for a week and ended up staying a month. So much to do here. BBQ, camp kitchen, clean amenities and just a 2 minute walk to the water. IGA close by with a take away store, with coles and Woolworths within a 5 minute drive. This park is operated by family members and make you part of their family. Very helpful and friendly to ensure you have a comfortable stay for your family holiday. Thank you Rio Rita, we will definitely be back!
Very clean! And warming! But no room to swing a cat 😺.! LoL

After what can only be described as the best 9 day Tour of Canberra Hospital. It was time to run and get to Batemans Bay. We booked Rio Rita months ago and after hearing or drama unfold, wow, nothing was a problem. I’m pretty sure I had them on speed dial with bi daily updates. Anyway, this CP is in the best location, forget the big guys because you get way better service. Amenities might be dated, but you have never seen any cleaner. Thanks Guys. We can’t thank you enough for all you did for us. If we ever are back down your way, we’ll be sure to stop in again.
Spent 5 days here loved it very close to everything you need park very well run nice people very helpful would stay here again no problem

Stayed here for two nights in our van and honestly - we could have stayed for the whole month! The hospitality from Debbie and Donna was so welcoming, the amenities are very clean and the views  were beautiful. Can’t wait to stay again!
We stayed at Rio over New Year for 10 days. Had a power caravan site.  Very friendly and helpful staff.  Bathrooms kept very neat and tidy.  Walking distance to town and great fishing.
We were put at the front of the yard so had a great view. The staff were friendly and helpful. Amenities are old but clean. Would definitely stay there again.
Good caravan park, some kid tried to race me in his push bike

Look, it’s not the best for kids regarding facilities. But it’s very chill, open areas with beautiful grassy sites. If you’re up for making your own fun then it’s a great place. Toilets are dated but well maintained.

This a great park, and management great. If you have any questions or concerns they on it immediately. Friendly, professional and very helpful. We had a great stay and will be back. To the management, thank you for making our stay a stay to remember. Will recommend the park to everyone.

Me and my girlfriend stayed in studio 1 of the tiny homes at rio rita and absolutely loved it. The self check in was so convenient for us and we have no complaints about the room, location or facilities!
Spent a weekend with my friends at this park. The staff were so accommodating and the amenities were top notch. Will definitely be coming back.

We stayed for 2 nights, we travel with our 2 kelpies, excellent location almost right on the beach, short walk over the bridge to large shopping complex (coles, reject shop, cafes, fish and chips).Quiet site although right on the road, ladies and mens amenties clean nice hot showers, $45 per night probably about $10 per night too much.
Friendly staff would stay here again.
Mowed site spaces. Some non powered and a few powered sites. Clean facilities. Reasonably quiet. Close to town. Friendly Manager.

The camp entry was on the left and as we drove in we saw all permanent caravans. We headed past to the back area which was grassed. It was mowed, marked, clean and tidy. Toilet block with showers and there were picnic tables undercover.
In the morning we went to the office but there was no one there.
Great overnight camp ground.
